title: Pelvic injuries: iliac spine avulsion (AIIS)
body_html:
Anterior inferior iliac spine (AIIS) avulsion fracture

Mechanism
  • due to eccentric contraction of the rectus femoris (active muscle lengthening under load)
  • typically seen in adolescents 14-17
  • occurs most often in sports involving kicking

Clinical features
  • patient may hear pop or snap at time of injury
  • antalgic gait
  • anterior hip pain and hip flexion weakness

Aetiology
  • AIIS site of attachment for rectus femoris

Management
  • Conservative typically: crutches, analgesics, and modified activity
  • Recovery time usually 4-6 weeks.

Image sourced from Wikipedia
